ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA

DISCOVERY 612, HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA IS GRANTED AS THE SUBPOENA IS OVERLY BROAD. DEFENDANT IS ADVISED THAT THE LIMITATIONS SUGGESTED BY PLAINTIFF ARE PROPER. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION IS MADE WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST DEFENDAT IN THE AMOUNT OF $675, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Feb-16-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Charles Owen Geerhart, appeared for Plaintiff/moving party Devin Graham
Andrew J. Lautenbach, appeared for Defendant/Oppossing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Quash Subpoena is granted as the subpoena is overly broad. Defendant is advised that the limitations suggested by Plaintiff are proper. The court finds that Defendant's opposition is made without substantial justification. Sanctions are imposed against Defendat in the amount of $675, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-27-2005 Assigned To Arbitration On Mar-06-06; Pre-Arbitration Settlement Conference On Apr-03-06 At 01:30 P.M.
